_comment_top: R6 Wave 8 — seed row for the CodeInterpreterHandler 'AnalyzeData' method. Replaces one of the two legacy hardcoded LLM functions previously registered on CodeInterpreterTools (removed from SprkChatAgentFactory.ResolveTools lines ~1066-1105).
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'CodeInterpreterHandler'. The handler reads sprk_configuration.method = 'AnalyzeData' to dispatch to the tabular/CSV data-analysis pipeline.
_comment_method_dispatch: Two rows (CODE-ANALYZE / CODE-CHART) share one handler class — same pattern as KnowledgeRetrievalHandler (Wave 7c) and TextRefinementHandler (Wave 7 Q9). Each row's sprk_configuration.method discriminator selects the internal method.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = 'code_interpreter' (= PlaybookCapabilities.CodeInterpreter) gates this tool to playbooks whose capability set includes 'code_interpreter'. Standalone chat (no playbook, capabilities = CoreCapabilities) does NOT include 'code_interpreter', so this handler is unreachable from standalone chat — preserving the pre-Wave-8 security boundary that the hardcoded `if (capabilities.Contains(PlaybookCapabilities.CodeInterpreter))` block enforced. Per R6 Wave 7b infrastructure (commit 66da08ca), SprkChatAgentFactory.ResolveTools applies IsCapabilityGateSatisfied at session start and silently withholds rows whose RequiredCapability is not in the current playbook's capability set.
_comment_adr018: ADR-018 kill switch (CodeInterpreterOptions.Enabled) is enforced inside the handler before every sandbox call. When disabled, the handler returns a successful ToolResult with a user-readable unavailability string in data.message so the AI model can gracefully inform the user. This is per-invocation enforcement — separate from the capability gate above (which is per-playbook authorization).
_comment_adr016: ADR-016 rate limiting via static SemaphoreSlim bounded by CodeInterpreterOptions.MaxConcurrency is enforced inside the handler (preserves the pre-Wave-8 design where CodeInterpreterTools owned the gate, since the bridge does not re-check). The gate is shared across all handler instances in the same BFF process.
_comment_adr015: ADR-015 data governance: only caller-supplied 'data' excerpts are forwarded to the sandbox. The handler does NOT auto-fetch external resources. Telemetry logs lengths + counts + bucket labels only — never the data or the question content.
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. The exact phrasing below is preserved verbatim from the legacy hardcoded registration so the LLM's tool-selection behavior is unchanged after migration.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode + sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Analyze tabular or CSV data to answer a specific question. Use this when the user wants statistics, trends, or comparisons derived from structured data. Supply only the relevant data excerpt — never full documents or PII.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. The legacy CodeInterpreterTools was registered exclusively for chat; the 11 production node executors (NFR-08) do not include a Code Interpreter executor, so playbook orchestration has no call path. CodeInterpreterHandler.SupportedInvocationContexts matches (InvocationContextKind.Chat) and ExecuteAsync (playbook) returns a defensive validation error.

sprk_jsonschema.title: CodeInterpreterHandler.AnalyzeData Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the AnalyzeData method. The 'data' field carries a caller-supplied tabular / CSV excerpt; 'question' is the analysis question to answer. Both fields are required.

data.description: CSV or tabular data excerpt to analyze. MUST be the smallest excerpt that answers the question — full documents, raw file bytes, or data containing PII MUST NOT be passed (ADR-015 data governance).

question.description: Specific analysis question to answer using the data (e.g., 'what is the average value across Q1?', 'which row has the highest revenue?').

sprk_configuration._comment: method = 'AnalyzeData' is the dispatch discriminator. This row exposes only the data-analysis method.
